channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: PDO signs six-year EPC framework with Petrojet-ENPPI
item.description: Petroleum Development Oman (PDO) has signed a six-year engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) framework agreement with the Petrojet-ENPPI Consortium.

item.title: Petronas Bags New Contract to Supply LNG to Shizuoka Gas
item.description: Malaysia's national oil and gas company signed a new agreement to supply about 0.84 million metric tons of liquefied natural gas to Japanese utility Shizuoka Gas.

item.title: ConocoPhillips to acquire 42% stake in bp’s Kirkuk venture
item.description: ConocoPhillips has reached an agreement with BP to acquire a 42% stake in BP Energy Company of Kirkuk Limited (BP ECKL) to join the ongoing redevelopment of major oil fields in the Kirkuk region in northern Iraq.

item.title: US Strikes after Troops Killed as Iran Quits Deal
item.description: The U.S. and Iran engaged in a quickening series of tit-for-tat attacks, with the American military conducting a wave of strikes on the Islamic Republic following the deaths of service personnel.

item.title: Tankers Fear Hormuz Transit as Iran Stops Vessels
item.description: An empty Malta-flagged, Greece-managed oil products tanker halted at anchor in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman early on Monday as vessel owners and operators grow increasingly wary of attempting to transit the Strait in…

item.title: China’s Fuel Oil Exports Hit 2026 High as Shipping Demand Rebounds
item.description: China exported 577,000 barrels daily of fuel oil last month, which was the most since the start of the year and an 18% increase from June 2025, Reuters reported today, citing official customs data. At the same time…

item.title: LNG Shipments Through Strait of Hormuz Grind to a Halt as Conflict Escalates
item.description: Exports of liquefied natural gas via the Strait of Hormuz appear to have ground to a halt over the past three days, data from tanker-tracking firms have suggested, while oil tanker traffic has shrunk considerably…

item.title: Gulf Oil Exports Briefly Recovered to Pre-War Levels Before Latest Escalation
item.description: Crude oil and condensate exports from the Persian Gulf rose to pre-war levels in the first half of July, data from Kpler and Vortexa shows, as cited by Reuters, reaching between 12 and 13.6 million barrels daily. The…

item.title: Did Scientists Just Solve The Biggest Mystery Holding Back Solid-State Batteries
item.description: Scientists now know why solid-state batteries have repeatedly failed. Two separate studies have identified the physical processes responsible for the short circuits that have delayed commercialization for years, turning…

item.title: Europe Was Supposed to Run Out of Jet Fuel by June. It Didn’t
item.description: Early warnings surrounding the Strait of Hormuz suggested that European airports and fuel markets could face physical shortages by the beginning of summer. Those shortages largely failed to materialise, revealing both…

item.title: What Are Mineral Rights? The Complete Guide to Owning, Leasing and Selling Them
item.description: Millions of Americans own oil and gas mineral rights and have no real idea what that means. Some inherited a folder of yellowed documents from a grandparent's estate. Some bought a piece of land and assumed everything…

item.title: Amundi Says Oil, Gas Crucial to EU Clean Energy Transition
item.description: Amundi wants the EU to free asset managers to add oil and gas exposures to a new fund category intended to support the transition to a lower-carbon economy.

item.title: Europe's Heatwave Is Becoming an Energy Crisis
item.description: The intense early summer heatwave in Europe is warming the biggest rivers and drying up their water levels, disrupting energy deliveries, power generation, and key supply chain routes. The heatwaves in Europe started as…

item.title: What Is a Microgrid? How They Work and Why They're Suddenly Everywhere
item.description: When Hurricane Maria flattened Puerto Rico's power grid in 2017, one building in the mountain town of Adjuntas never went dark. Casa Pueblo, a local environmental group, had already wired its headquarters with solar…

item.title: Canada Unveils New Pipeline to Cut Reliance on U.S. Oil Routes
item.description: A new oil pipeline could connect eastern and western Canada as the oil-rich country seeks to become more self-reliant and reduce its energy dependence on the United States. However, not everyone is in support of the…

item.title: Forgotten Shale Gas Play Reemerges as Oil Hot Spot
item.description: Companies including Obsidian Energy Ltd. and Yangarra Resources Corp. are breathing new life into a long-dormant gas reservoir - except now they're drilling for oil.

item.title: Oil Jumps on Fears of Wider Iran War
item.description: Traders boosted bullish oil bets as geopolitical tensions increased risks to global energy and fuel markets.

item.title: Bangladesh's $12.65 Billion Nuclear Bet Faces Its Biggest Test Yet
item.description: The world is turning toward nuclear power as a solution to the increasingly dire energy trilemma – the tricky issue of sourcing energy that is simultaneously affordable, sustainable, and secure. As the artificial…

item.title: Colombia's New Government Prepares Fossil Fuel Comeback
item.description: On 21st June, conservative, Trump-endorsed presidential candidate Abelardo de la Espriella won the election by just 1 per cent over Iván Cepeda, who was expected to take over the Socialist Party leadership from current…

item.title: Chevron, Cheniere Reps Join DOE SEAB Board
item.description: The U.S. Department of Energy announced newly appointed members of the Secretary of Energy Advisory Board.
